title: Day Of The Jackanapes

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car (inferred from tire screeching)
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase expressing pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase in expected context - reliable but not innovative

jokes.text: Oh, Doctor, I'm crazy! [Crying]
jokes.setup: Patient at doctor's office
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic declaration of insanity
jokes.explanation: Generic crazy patient trope with minimal setup or payoff

jokes.text: And you stalled for 20 minutes. Yes. Yes, I did. I was told to.
jokes.setup: Game show host pointing out contestant's delay tactic
jokes.punchline: Moe admits he was instructed to stall, breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about game show production tactics

jokes.text: Well, it all starts when a 'nulecule' comes out of its nest.
jokes.setup: Moe calls Homer for nuclear physics help
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of nuclear physics with made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism - 'nulecule' and 'nest' show his childlike misunderstanding

jokes.text: The answer is bonbon!
jokes.setup: Complex nuclear physics question
jokes.punchline: Homer confidently declares candy as the answer
jokes.explanation: Absurd confidence in wrong answer perfectly matches Homer's character

jokes.text: Bonbon, eh, Moe? Is that your ultimate response? - Yeah-huh. - Ohh. You are... correct!
jokes.setup: Obviously wrong answer to physics question
jokes.punchline: It's somehow correct, defying logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - the absurd answer being correct subverts expectations brilliantly

jokes.text: Aw, this quiz show crap is just a fad.
jokes.setup: Quiz shows are dominating TV ratings
jokes.punchline: Krusty dismisses hugely popular format as temporary
jokes.explanation: Typical showbiz denial of changing times - fits Krusty's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: All we're saying is be dangerous, but warm. And edgy-cute.
jokes.setup: Network trying to give creative direction
jokes.punchline: Contradictory, meaningless corporate buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of TV executive speak - 'edgy-cute' is perfectly contradictory

jokes.text: These ferkachted network notes. Can you believe this?
jokes.setup: Krusty dealing with network interference
jokes.punchline: Uses Yiddish curse word 'ferkachted' (messed up)
jokes.explanation: Authentic Krusty character moment using Yiddish - shows his heritage and frustration

jokes.text: I don't need 12 suits telling me which way to pee.
jokes.setup: Krusty's frustration with network micromanagement
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor about being told how to do basic bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Vivid metaphor for creative interference - crude but effective

jokes.text: For 'pee,' could you substitute 'whiz'? I don't know. That could upset the Cheez Whiz people.
jokes.setup: Executive asking for euphemism substitution
jokes.punchline: Absurd concern about offending cheese product company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of corporate absurdity - the Cheez Whiz concern is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: You can kill me, but two more will take my place!
jokes.setup: Executive being physically attacked
jokes.punchline: Corporate hydra metaphor - killing one creates more
jokes.explanation: Clever hydra metaphor for corporate bureaucracy - relatable workplace horror

jokes.text: Well, I think it's good for a show to go off the air before it becomes stale and repetitive.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on Krusty's retirement
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on The Simpsons itself becoming long-running
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware joke - Lisa advocating for shows ending before declining, while The Simpsons continues indefinitely

jokes.text: Maggie shot Mr. Burns again! [Cricket Chirping]
jokes.setup: Marge treating shocking violence as mundane
jokes.punchline: References famous 'Who Shot Mr. Burns' episodes as routine occurrence
jokes.explanation: Great callback treating iconic episode event as repetitive - cricket chirping sells the awkward silence

jokes.text: not seen since the small plane crash that killed the Banana Splits.
jokes.setup: Comparing outpouring of anguish over Krusty
jokes.punchline: References fictional tragic death of children's TV characters
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about children's entertainers dying tragically - references real '70s show

jokes.text: Hey, Kent, are you guys still having that contest where we guess what city the weather girl's in? - [Chuckles] That was eight years ago. - Is it Pittsburgh?
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking about TV contest
jokes.punchline: Contest ended eight years ago but he's still guessing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum obliviousness - still participating in long-ended contest

jokes.text: Kent, the young people today, they think comedy is dirty words. It's not. It's words that sound dirty, like 'mukluk.'
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining what real comedy is
jokes.punchline: 'Mukluk' (Eskimo boot) sounds dirty but isn't
jokes.explanation: Good comedy theory joke - mukluk does sound vaguely dirty for innocent word

jokes.text: Oh, you know, I had a thing for Judge Judy and blank tape was 3.99. What would you do?
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining why he taped over his old shows
jokes.punchline: Chose Judge Judy over preserving his own career legacy for economic reasons
jokes.explanation: Relatable prioritization of cheap entertainment over personal history

jokes.text: The show didn't really get funny until we fired Sideshow Bob and hired whozits.
jokes.setup: Krusty dismissing his old episodes as unfunny
jokes.punchline: Can't even remember name of Bob's replacement
jokes.explanation: Shows Krusty's casual dismissal of people and inability to remember names

jokes.text: Oh, honestly. At Chino they get to stay up till 9:00.
jokes.setup: Prison lights-out announcement
jokes.punchline: Bob complaining about bedtime like it's a hotel amenity comparison
jokes.explanation: Bob's pretentious prison standards - treating maximum security like hospitality comparison

jokes.text: consists mainly of prisoners who club me with books.
jokes.setup: Bob complaining about cruel prison conditions
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'book club'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect setup/payoff structure and Bob's intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: These are from the new Tom Clancy. Although it's less painful than reading him. Am I right, folks?
jokes.setup: Bob showing injuries from book-clubbing
jokes.punchline: Literary criticism while being beaten - Clancy books are painful to read
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bob moment - making literary criticism while being violently beaten, appeals to audience

jokes.text: Hey, no fair. You got long legs. I got these little bitty hooves.
jokes.setup: Lou chasing Bob who's walking fast
jokes.punchline: Police officer refers to his own feet as 'hooves'
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating body humor from minor character - 'hooves' is amusingly specific

jokes.text: You want to live in the box? Cost you two bucks a day.
jokes.setup: Bob being shown his storage unit
jokes.punchline: Storage facility employee casually offers box as living quarters for rent
jokes.explanation: Casual entrepreneurship in face of homelessness - treats human habitation as business opportunity

jokes.text: Revenge, huh? Half the guys in here are working on that.
jokes.setup: Bob mentions his revenge scheme
jokes.punchline: Neighbor treats elaborate revenge plots as commonplace storage unit activity
jokes.explanation: Casual attitude toward criminal schemes - normalizes Bob's behavior through mundane reaction

jokes.text: To be fair, most of those people were Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: Principal questioning Bob's murder attempts
jokes.punchline: Bob justifies attempted murders as being focused on one victim
jokes.explanation: Bob's logical defense of his criminal record - treats multiple murder attempts as targeting efficiency

jokes.text: Good luck. That kid's like the Road Runner. He won't go down.
jokes.setup: Skinner responding to Bob's Bart obsession
jokes.punchline: Compares Bart to cartoon character known for escaping elaborate traps
jokes.explanation: Apt comparison positioning Bart as Road Runner to Bob's Wile E. Coyote

jokes.text: Quel dommage.
jokes.setup: French Club picnic being canceled
jokes.punchline: Bob responds in perfect French ('What a shame')
jokes.explanation: Shows Bob's intellect and pretension - responds to school announcement in French

jokes.text: I believe the clan is Graham-Monroe.
jokes.setup: Announcing found plaid kilt
jokes.punchline: Bob knows obscure Scottish clan patterns
jokes.explanation: Bob's unnecessary expertise in Scottish heraldry shows his pretentious knowledge

jokes.text: Hey, I'm not afraid of you. Every time we tangle, you wind up in jail. I'm 6 and 0.
jokes.setup: Bob expecting Bart to be scared
jokes.punchline: Bart treats their encounters like sports statistics
jokes.explanation: Great callback acknowledging their history while showing Bart's confidence through sports metaphor

jokes.text: Rakes. My old archenemy. I thought I was your archenemy.
jokes.setup: Bob encountering rakes in shed
jokes.punchline: References his famous rake-stepping scenes, Bart feels replaced
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to iconic rake-stepping scene - Bart's jealousy over being replaced by garden tools

jokes.text: I have a life outside of you, Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart assuming he's Bob's only focus
jokes.punchline: Bob casually mentions having other nemeses
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Bart's ego - Bob has diversified his hatred

jokes.text: Krusty? That's the one man I would never kill!
jokes.setup: Bob's plan to have Bart kill Krusty
jokes.punchline: Bart's genuine love for Krusty makes plan impossible
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's moral limits and genuine affection for his hero

jokes.text: That's it. Oh, damn it! Where'd it go? I needed that for laundry!
jokes.setup: Bob trying to hypnotize Bart with shiny quarter
jokes.punchline: Drops quarter and breaks character worrying about mundane laundry needs
jokes.explanation: Humanizing Bob moment - elaborate villain worried about basic living expenses

jokes.text: Let it entrance you with its twirliness. Twirliness.
jokes.setup: Bob attempting hypnosis with spinning device
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'twirliness' delivered with pretentious authority
jokes.explanation: Bob's verbose nature creating unnecessary words - 'twirliness' sounds official but is made up

jokes.text: I didn't say anything about command. You're in my power. Say so. I am in your power. Excellent. Actually, go back to command. No, power. Power.
jokes.setup: Bob giving hypnotic commands
jokes.punchline: Gets sidetracked by semantic precision during crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob character moment - his intellectual pretension sabotages his own evil plan

jokes.text: If anyone asks, you were at the flower shop. I was at the flower shop. Oh, yeah. Uh, I was at the flower shop too. Yep, getting drunk at the old flower shop.
jokes.setup: Bob giving Bart an alibi
jokes.punchline: Homer overhears and creates ridiculous version about drinking at flower shop
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent alibi to Homer's absurd interpretation of flower shop as bar

jokes.text: Yes! Yes! Work the groin!
jokes.setup: Bob watching Bart practice baseball
jokes.punchline: Encourages violent techniques while imagining Bart attacking Krusty
jokes.explanation: Moderately uncomfortable humor from Bob's violent enthusiasm

jokes.text: These days, it's all aluminum this and George W. That.
jokes.setup: Praising wooden baseball bats
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical complaint mixing aluminum bats with George W. Bush
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's confused complaint structure - 'aluminum this and George W. that' makes no sense

jokes.text: Someone please give me a job. I've lowered my quote to eight million.
jokes.setup: Wolfcastle appearing as out-of-work actor
jokes.punchline: Desperate for work but still demanding huge salary
jokes.explanation: Good actor desperation humor - 'lowering' to still-astronomical amount

jokes.text: Look at me. I'm Kaputnik, the Russian satellite. [Gasps] Oh, the Bolshoi's doing the Nutcracker in my pants!
jokes.setup: Early TV appearance doing space-themed act
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre about ballet in his underwear
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate risqué humor that got Krusty in trouble - mildly clever wordplay

jokes.text: Back then you couldn't say 'pants' on TV. I was banned for 10 years.
jokes.setup: Krusty's racy joke from flashback
jokes.punchline: Absurdly strict censorship standards of the past
jokes.explanation: Good historical comedy about changing broadcast standards - 'pants' being forbidden is absurd

jokes.text: The ferkachted doors are stuck! Don't just stare, Arte. Help me!
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to make Laugh-In appearance
jokes.punchline: Stuck in iconic doors, breaks character to ask for help
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback to Laugh-In show format with Krusty's Yiddish cursing

jokes.text: Oh, look. It was all a bookkeeping snafu.
jokes.setup: Called out for not donating promised million dollars to charity
jokes.punchline: Dismisses major fraud as minor accounting error
jokes.explanation: Typical celebrity excuse for not following through on charity promises

jokes.text: Ah, kettle chips. The perfect side dish for revenge.
jokes.setup: Bob preparing for his revenge plot
jokes.punchline: Treats snack food as essential component of villainy
jokes.explanation: Bob's pretentious nature applied to snack selection - treating mundane choice as part of grand scheme

jokes.text: Well, Krusty, this is your Waterloo. Soon you'll be Napoléon Blownapart. Ew. Terrible.
jokes.setup: Bob making dramatic declaration about defeating Krusty
jokes.punchline: Makes terrible pun, immediately gets called out for bad wordplay
jokes.explanation: Self-aware bad pun immediately criticized - Bob's pretension deflated by honest reaction

jokes.text: Ew. Terrible. Oh, hush up, Leo!
jokes.setup: Bob makes bad Napoleon pun
jokes.punchline: Even he knows it's bad, tells imaginary critic to shut up
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke where Bob critiques his own bad pun and argues with conscience

jokes.text: Sideshow Bob, I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Krusty's final show speech about regrets
jokes.punchline: Sincere apology to his attempted murderer
jokes.explanation: Touching moment that derails Bob's revenge - simple, heartfelt apology has more power than elaborate schemes

jokes.text: Oh, no! My boy bomb!
jokes.setup: Krusty's apology moves Bob to tears
jokes.punchline: Bob realizes his emotional response ruined his murder plot
jokes.explanation: Bob's humanity sabotages his evil plan - 'boy bomb' shows his twisted terminology while revealing plot failure

jokes.text: Just some network executives. [Deep Voices] We have notes. Have you thought about Dave Chappelle? Destroy!
jokes.setup: Asked if anyone was hurt in the explosion
jokes.punchline: Only network executives hurt, who continue giving notes while dying
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy treating executive deaths as no loss - they literally give notes while dying, perfect industry satire

jokes.text: Will ya stop with the 'sorry'? Every time you try to kill me, my ratings go through the roof, you nut!
jokes.setup: Krusty apologizing for Bob's attempted murder
jokes.punchline: Krusty reveals he benefits from assassination attempts
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Krusty's cynical showbiz attitude - benefits from attempted murders

jokes.text: We are good together, Krusty. It makes me sad that you're getting the death penalty.
jokes.setup: Bob and Krusty recognizing their partnership
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of death penalty as minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating execution as relationship obstacle rather than serious consequence

jokes.text: Okay, where do you wanna do this thing? Isn't it customary to have a trial? Oh, a wise guy, huh?
jokes.setup: Police ready to execute Bob immediately
jokes.punchline: Bob's reasonable request for due process treated as attitude problem
jokes.explanation: Absurd police efficiency bypassing legal system - asking for trial is seen as being difficult

jokes.text: Mukluk.
jokes.setup: Final moment of episode
jokes.punchline: Returns to Krusty's earlier comedy theory about words that sound dirty
jokes.explanation: Nice callback ending tying back to earlier comedy philosophy - simple but effective closure

tmdb_overview: Krusty announces his retirement due to interference from network executives and the growing popularity of the big-money game show "Me Wantee", but when Krusty reveals to Sideshow Bob that all of the episodes featuring him have been erased, Sideshow Bob uses Krusty's biggest fan (Bart) to murder him during Krusty's farewell show.
